prompt:
Every cache-miss on our manifest service falls through to a slow object-store HEAD, and ~90% of those keys have never existed. I want a bloom filter in front of it. No third-party deps — this goes in a repo where the security folks veto every new module.

Build package bloom at the root of module go-bloomset:

  func New(bits, hashes int) (*Filter, error)
  func (f *Filter) Add(key string)
  func (f *Filter) MaybeContains(key string) bool
  func (f *Filter) EstimatedFill() float64
  func (f *Filter) Union(other *Filter) error
  func (f *Filter) Bits() int
  func (f *Filter) Hashes() int
  var ErrShapeMismatch = errors.New(...)

What I care about:
- New validates: bits and hashes must both be positive, otherwise a non-nil error.
- Hashing must be fully deterministic — no per-process random seed. We serialize these filters between replicas eventually, so two filters with the same shape and the same keys must answer identically. Derive your k bit positions from deterministic hash functions (FNV variants from hash/fnv with double hashing is fine).
- The cardinal rule of a bloom filter: NEVER a false negative. Added keys always report maybe-present. False positives allowed but should be rare at sane sizing — the test suite checks dispersion on a 16k-bit filter.
- EstimatedFill returns the fraction of bits currently set (0 for a fresh filter, at most 1). Re-adding a key sets nothing new.
- Union OR-s another filter of the exact same shape (same bits AND same hashes) into the receiver. Shape mismatch: return ErrShapeMismatch and leave the receiver completely untouched — no partial merge. The argument is never modified either way.

bloom_test.go is the acceptance contract; leave it alone. Done means go test -race -timeout 30s ./... passes.
